senses.definition: to perform extremely well in something, especially a test

examples.x: Despite the difficult questions, she managed to ace the final exam with a perfect score.
The dedicated student studied diligently and was able to ace the challenging physics test.
He consistently aces his presentations by thoroughly preparing and delivering with confidence.
The athlete trained rigorously and managed to ace the competition, winning a gold medal.
With focused preparation, the job candidate aced the interview and secured the position.

senses.definition: to give a responsibility or job to someone

examples.x: The manager decided to appoint a new team leader to oversee the project.
The president has the authority to appoint individuals to key positions within the organization.
It's common for companies to appoint experienced managers to lead crucial departments.
The president has recently appointed a new advisor to the team.
The experienced manager appointed specific roles during a period of organizational change.

senses.definition: to work with someone else in order to create something or reach the same goal

examples.x: The researchers collaborated on a groundbreaking study in neuroscience.
Artists from different backgrounds collaborated on a mural for the community center.
We need to collaborate with our colleagues to finalize the project proposal.
The two companies decided to collaborate on developing a new software application.
Teachers and parents collaborated to organize a successful school fundraiser.

senses.definition: to start happening or being

examples.x: The ceremony will commence at 10 AM sharp.
The construction work commenced early in the morning.
The ceremony commenced with a speech from the mayor.
As soon as everyone is seated, the play will commence.
The meeting commenced with the chairman's opening remarks.

senses.definition: to investigate further based on information or suggestions provided by someone

examples.x: After the seminar, I decided to follow up on the speaker's research and findings.
I'll follow up on the leads you provided and gather more details about the job openings.
The detective decided to follow up on the witness's testimony to gather additional evidence.
The supervisor asked me to follow up on the progress of the project with the team.

senses.definition: to simultaneously do more than one thing

examples.x: In her busy job, she has to multitask efficiently to handle emails, phone calls, and meetings throughout the day.
Parents often need to multitask, balancing work responsibilities with household chores and caring for their children.
During the team meeting, he demonstrated his ability to multitask by responding to messages while actively participating in the discussion.
The chef had to multitask in the kitchen, preparing multiple dishes at the same time to meet the demands of a busy restaurant.

senses.definition: to arrange or put off an activity or an event for a later time than its original schedule

examples.x: They postponed the meeting until next week due to scheduling conflicts.
She postponed her vacation because of unexpected work commitments.
He postponed the project deadline to allow more time for preparation.
We are postponing our trip until next month.
I will postpone my dentist appointment until after my vacation.

senses.definition: to employ people for a company, etc.

examples.x: The company is actively recruiting new graduates for entry-level positions.
They decided to recruit experienced professionals to enhance the team's expertise.
During the hiring process, it's crucial to recruit candidates who align with the company's values.
Start-ups often face challenges when recruiting talent due to budget constraints.
Companies use various strategies to recruit top talent in competitive industries.

senses.definition: to officially announce one's departure from a job, position, etc.

examples.x: She decided to resign from her position as CEO.
He resigned from his job to pursue other opportunities.
They resigned from the board of directors due to conflicts of interest.
The mayor announced his decision to resign from office.
They resigned from the committee in protest of the decision.

senses.definition: extremely busy and chaotic

examples.x: The office was always hectic during the end of the month with deadlines approaching.
They managed to complete the project despite the hectic pace of the last few weeks.
The holiday season was particularly hectic for retail employees.
She had a hectic schedule with meetings, deadlines, and constant travel.
The last-minute changes made the event planning even more hectic than usual.

senses.definition: (in business) concentrating on or using something a lot, such as a piece of equipment, etc.
boring because of being the same thing all the time

examples.x: The endless hours of data entry made her workday feel monotonous and never-ending.
The monotonous droning of the lecturer's voice put many students to sleep during the long lecture.
The monotonous landscape of the desert seemed to stretch on forever without any notable features.
The monotonous routine of his daily life left him yearning for excitement and new experiences.
The repetitive tasks at the assembly line made the job monotonous and uninteresting.

senses.definition: (of an activity) between only two people
causing excitement

examples.x: The stimulating discussion at the conference sparked new ideas among the attendees.
She found the challenging puzzles in the book to be intellectually stimulating.
The museum's interactive exhibits provided a stimulating experience for visitors of all ages.
He enjoyed the stimulating atmosphere of the bustling city.

senses.definition: boring and repetitive, often causing frustration or weariness due to a lack of variety or interest

examples.x: The tedious task of filing paperwork made the afternoon drag on.
The tedious process of data entry required hours of concentration and attention to detail.
The tedious commute to work in heavy traffic was a daily ordeal for many city dwellers.
Listening to the lecturer drone on about the same topic for hours became tedious after a while.
Sorting through the clutter in the attic proved to be a tedious and time-consuming endeavor.

senses.definition: (of a person) not having much work to do in their job or being unable to use their full potential

examples.x: Despite her qualifications, she felt underemployed in her current job, which didn't utilize her full skill set.
The underemployed workforce seeks opportunities for career advancement and better utilization of their skills.
The underemployed population often seeks opportunities for career advancement or additional training.

senses.definition: a room where the board of directors meet

examples.x: The CEO called a meeting in the boardroom to discuss the company's quarterly earnings.
The boardroom was equipped with a large conference table and comfortable chairs for the directors.
They presented their proposal to the board of directors in the spacious boardroom.
The boardroom overlooked the city skyline, offering a breathtaking view during meetings.
Important decisions about company strategy are often made in the boardroom.

senses.definition: a period when a student or graduate works, often unpaid, in order to meet some requirements to qualify for something or to gain work-related experience

examples.x: She completed an internship at a local hospital to gain practical experience in nursing.
The company offers summer internships for college students interested in marketing.
He secured an internship at a law firm to learn about legal procedures firsthand.
Internships are valuable opportunities for students to apply classroom knowledge in real-world settings.
The internship program provided him with valuable skills and insights into the industry.

senses.definition: a position or job that is available

examples.x: The company announced a vacancy for a senior marketing manager.
He applied for the vacancy in the accounting department.
The HR department is actively recruiting to fill several vacancies in the sales team.
She received an email notification about a vacancy for a software developer.
The newspaper advertisement listed several vacancies in customer service roles.

senses.definition: someone who works with someone else, having the same job

examples.x: My coworker helped me with a difficult project last week.
I enjoy collaborating with my coworkers on team projects.
She had lunch with her coworkers in the office cafeteria.
The meeting room was filled with coworkers discussing the new project.
My coworker received a promotion after years of hard work.

senses.definition: someone who observes or directs a person or an activity

examples.x: The supervisor oversees the daily operations of the department.
She reported her concerns to her supervisor regarding the project timeline.
The supervisor provided feedback and guidance to improve performance.
Our supervisor conducts regular meetings to discuss team progress.

senses.definition: someone who is not skilled or experienced enough for a specific activity

examples.x: She started as an amateur in photography but quickly developed her skills.
The competition was open to both amateurs and professionals.
He joined a local club to meet other amateurs interested in astronomy.
The workshop was designed for amateurs to learn basic cooking techniques.
As an amateur, he entered the race for the experience rather than aiming to win.

senses.definition: someone who answers the questions during an interview

examples.x: The interviewee arrived early for the job interview.
She prepared thoroughly to impress the interviewers as an interviewee.
The panel of interviewers asked challenging questions to each interviewee.
As an interviewee, he demonstrated his problem-solving skills effectively.
The interviewee's responses were well-received by the hiring committee.

senses.definition: a specific period of time granted to a person who is ill to temporary leave work

examples.x: She took sick leave to recover from a severe cold.
He applied for sick leave due to a medical emergency.
Employees are entitled to a certain number of sick leave days per year.
The company policy allows for paid sick leave for full-time employees.
She returned to work after her sick leave feeling much better.

senses.definition: a period of time when a woman can take a break from working and stay home before and after the birth of her child

examples.x: She went on maternity leave a month before her baby was due.
The company's maternity leave policy provides twelve weeks of paid time off for new mothers.
He took paternity leave to support his partner during her maternity leave.
After her maternity leave, she plans to return to work part-time initially.
Maternity leave allowed her to bond with her newborn without worrying about work responsibilities.

senses.definition: (of people) the ability to perform more than one task simultaneously

examples.x: Multitasking can sometimes lead to decreased productivity if not done carefully.

senses.definition: work, particularly difficult physical work

examples.x: The construction workers performed hours of hard labor under the scorching sun.
The farm relied on seasonal labor to harvest the crops efficiently.
The labor involved in building the new bridge required strength and endurance.
She hired additional labor to help with the extensive renovations on her house.

senses.definition: the practice of treating a person or different categories of people less fairly than others

examples.x: The company faced a lawsuit for discrimination against female employees.
Discrimination based on race, gender, or religion is illegal in many countries.
Efforts to reduce discrimination in the workplace include implementing diversity training programs.
The new policies aim to combat discrimination and promote equality for all employees.
She spoke out against discrimination after witnessing unfair treatment of her colleagues.

senses.definition: a regular payment made to a retired person by the government or a former employer

examples.x: My grandfather's pension allows him to travel during his retirement.
After working for the company for over thirty years, he was able to retire with a comfortable pension.
She was concerned about the security of her pension following the company's financial difficulties.
Government employees often receive a pension as part of their retirement benefits.

senses.definition: a letter written by a former employer about a former employee who has applied for a new job, giving information about them

examples.x: She requested a reference from her previous manager to support her job application.
His reference highlighted his strong work ethic and excellent communication skills.
The company requires at least two references from former employers before making a hiring decision.
Her glowing reference helped her secure the position she had applied for.
Before leaving her old job, she made sure to ask for a written reference from her supervisor.

senses.definition: a plan or timetable outlining the sequence of events or activities

examples.x: She checked her schedule to see when her next meeting was.
The project manager created a detailed schedule to ensure all tasks were completed on time.
He adjusted his schedule to accommodate the unexpected changes in his travel plans.
Students received their class schedules for the upcoming semester.
The construction company adhered to a strict schedule to finish the project ahead of the deadline.

senses.definition: all the individuals who work in a particular company, industry, country, etc.

examples.x: The company plans to expand its workforce by hiring an additional 200 employees this year.
The aging workforce poses a challenge for industries that rely on manual labor.
Training programs were implemented to improve the skills of the existing workforce.
The tech industry is known for its diverse and highly skilled workforce.
Economic growth is often influenced by the productivity and size of the workforce.

senses.definition: the amount of work that a person or organization has to do

examples.x: She was overwhelmed by her heavy workload and had to work late every night.
The manager redistributed the workload to ensure no one was overburdened.
After the new hire joined the team, everyone's workload became more manageable.
He asked for an assistant to help with his increasing workload.
Stress and burnout can result from consistently handling an excessive workload.

senses.definition: a formal statement or letter declaring that one intends to end an agreement, especially an employment or residential contract

examples.x: She handed in her two-week notice at work after accepting a new job offer.
The landlord received a notice from the tenant, indicating their intention to terminate the lease.
The company's policy requires employees to provide written notice when resigning from their positions.
He was taken by surprise when he received a notice of eviction from his apartment complex.
The contract stipulated that a 30-day notice must be given before canceling the service.

senses.definition: an increase in someone's salary that happens at regular intervals

examples.x: She received a salary increment after completing her professional development program.
The company offers annual increments to its employees based on performance.
The cost of living increment was announced to adjust wages for inflation.
He negotiated for a higher increment during his performance review.
We observed a steady increment in sales over the past quarter.

senses.definition: free service that people of the same profession provide for each other, especially common among physicians

examples.x: As a gesture of professional courtesy, the lawyer offered free legal advice to her colleague.
Physicians often extend professional courtesy to fellow doctors by waiving consultation fees.
The accountant provided professional courtesy by offering a discounted tax preparation service to her colleague.
In academia, professors may grant professional courtesy by reviewing each other's research papers without charge.
Professional courtesy among architects often includes sharing industry insights and best practices without charge.
